🏄 This Morning
Dee Why · Long Reef · Curl Curl
--:--:--
AEST
🌤️ Clean morning — light 5kt WSW offshore (9kt gusts) grooming the faces. Favourable SE 141° swell sits right in the Dee Why Point / Long Reef / Curl Curl sweet band. Forecaster: winds stay light most of the day, then trend S later with crumble & bump — surf the morning. Cold air though (~10–11°C at dawn).
🦈
No active shark alert confirmed for the Northern Beaches (Palm Beach → Manly). SharkSmart NSW live map feed could not be read this refresh — tap to check live detections before you paddle out. (Note: Surfline reported Bondi closed for shark sightings — that's the Eastern Beaches, outside this coverage.) Check live ↗
🌟 Best pick right now
Long Reef
⭐⭐⭐☆☆ 1–3ft · Waist to shoulder
Best size + energy of the 3 (1–3ft, 106kJ) and SE 141° wraps into the south reef. Light cross-shore barely touches the sheltered corner — that's the play. Mid-morning to the 11:50am low is prime.
5kts WSW · 106kJ · 71 / 100

Session Windows
BEST
Mid-morning — best size pick of the day. Most height (1–3ft) and energy (106kJ) of the three. Wind is cross-shore but only 5kt, so tuck into the south reef corner where the headland shelters it and SE 141° wraps in cleanest. Reef banks show better as the tide drops toward the 11:50am low.
10–12pm
Your usual slot: runs into the 11:50am 0.5m low — reef exposes and walls stand up. Still the most powerful of the three. Cross-shore stays light; the south reef corner holds the cleanest shape.
✅ Best size + energy of the 3 (1–3ft, 106kJ) and SE 141° wraps into the south reef. Light cross-shore barely touches the sheltered corner — that's the play. Mid-morning to the 11:50am low is prime.

Board + Fins Recommendation
Sharpeye Inferno 72
5'9 · 28.6L
FT Tri L
Pick of the day at Long Reef. 1–3ft at 106kJ — the most rideable wall on the beaches. The thruster gives drive and bite to attack the south reef corner and hold a line through any cross-shore texture.
Sharpeye Inferno FT
5'11 · 28.8L
H4 Quad L
If the bigger 3ft sets show on the reef, the FT's extra length + H4 quad gives more paddle and down-the-line speed to make the longer walls.
Wetsuit
🤿
Rip Curl Heatseeker 3/2mm
Water 19°C (your 17–20°C band). Sitting and waiting for reef sets in cold dawn air (~11°C), the 3/2 keeps you comfortable — Surfline's 2mm call underrates the winter chill.

🎯 Technique Tip — South Reef Corner
Most powerful surf of the three (106kJ, 1–3ft). Paddle into the south reef corner where SE 141° wraps and the wall holds cleanest out of the cross-shore. Take the drop committed and drive hard off the bottom on your backhand while the face is clean. Short-period 8s energy means walls can peak up and shut down fast — trim early, stay low and in the pocket rather than hunting a turn through a folding section.
🌊 View on Surfline
2
Curl Curl
⭐⭐⭐☆☆
1–3ft
›
Fair
Current Conditions
Primary Swell
3.8ft · 8s
SE 141° · Curly's sweet spot
Secondary Swell
1.2ft · 12s
SE 128° + 0.5ft 19s S
Wind
5kts WSW
Offshore · clean · 9kt gusts
Water / Air Temp
19°C / 11°C
Mild water, cold dawn air
Wave Energy
93 kJ
Mid of the 3
Consistency
73 / 100
Best consistency of the 3
Session Windows
BEST
Morning offshore window. Best consistency of the three (73/100) and Curly is a S/SE-swell magnet — it eats SE 141°. Light WSW is offshore here, grooming the south-end right peak into the most regular chest-high faces. Strong backup if Long Reef's reef is crowded.
10–12pm
Your usual slot: most consistent of the three (73/100) — Curly keeps producing on the SE swell, right into the 11:50am low. Offshore WSW keeps the south-end right clean and makeable, though it may start backing to S late in the slot.
✅ Best consistency of the 3 (73/100) and offshore — Curly eats SE 141°. The south-end right gives the most regular walls. Solid all-day option that suits your 10–12 slot best in its first hour.

Board + Fins Recommendation
Sharpeye Inferno 72
5'9 · 28.6L
Sharpeye Tri L
Pick for 1–3ft at 93kJ. The thruster gives bite for sharp frontside carves on the south-end right — SE 141° is Curly's sweet spot and sets up a proper face for a committed bottom turn.
JS Sub Zero
5'6 · 30.3L
MF Twin+1 M/L
When the smaller waist-high sets roll through between pulses — twin glide holds speed and links the softer short-period sections the thruster would stall on.
Wetsuit
🤿
Rip Curl Heatseeker 3/2mm
Water 19°C (your 17–20°C band) — the 3/2 keeps you warm hunting the consistent south-end sets through the cold winter dawn air (~11°C).

🎯 Technique Tip — South-End Right (Frontside)
SE 141° is Curl Curl's sweet spot — it pumps hardest into the south-end right. Take off on the steepest part of the peak where the swell bends in for the most vertical face, and let the offshore hold it up. Pop up instantly and drive down the line. Short-period 8s energy means waves peak fast — commit one early carve off the top rather than forcing turns through a closing section.
🌊 View on Surfline
3
Dee Why
⭐⭐⭐☆☆
1–2ft+
›
Fair
Current Conditions
Primary Swell
3.8ft · 8s
SE 141° · favourable angle
Secondary Swell
1.2ft · 12s
SE 128° + 0.5ft 19s S
Wind
5kts WSW
Offshore · clean · 9kt gusts
Water / Air Temp
19°C / 11°C
Mild water, cold dawn air
Wave Energy
85 kJ
Softest of the 3 spots
Consistency
72 / 100
Fairly consistent
Session Windows
BEST
From first light (6:33am) through mid-morning. Light WSW offshore is cleanest early before it backs to S later and adds bump. Tide is filling off the 6:30am 1.0m high — the smaller, softer Point peaks favour a bit more water under them, so the earlier part of the morning is sweetest before the 11:50am low drains it.
10–12pm
Your usual slot: runs into the 11:50am 0.5m low — the Point right gets shallow and quick at the bottom of the tide. Winds likely still light but starting to trend S. First half (10–11am) is the better call before the tide bottoms out.
✅ Swell direction is on your side: SE 141° wraps into Dee Why Point. The catch today is size — softest of the three at 85kJ / 1–2ft+, so expect quick low-push peaks. Clean though.

Board + Fins Recommendation
JS Sub Zero
5'6 · 30.3L
MF Twin+1 M/L
Top pick for 1–2ft+ at just 85kJ — the softest, weakest of the three. The extra foam and twin glide carry you through the gutless short-period 8s sections a thruster would bog in. Paddle power for the small clean stuff.
Sharpeye Inferno 72
5'9 · 28.6L
Sharpeye Tri L
If a cleaner set peak lines up on the offshore at the Point, the thruster gives bite for one sharp frontside snap before the face flattens. Bring it as the better-sets option.
Wetsuit
🤿
Rip Curl Heatseeker 3/2mm
Water 19°C (your 17–20°C band). Surfline says 2mm, but with dawn air only ~10–11°C the 3/2 is the comfortable call for a longer session.
Tides — Blue Fish Point

6:30am
Low

11:50am
High
1.6m
6:41pm
🎯 Technique Tip — Frontside Point Right
Small, short-period (8s) and soft at 85kJ — peaks pop up fast with little push. Sit deep near the rock and use your strong paddle to get in early; the wave won't do the work for you. Pop up instantly, set a low fast line and generate your own speed with two quick pumps before committing one frontside snap. Don't wait for a pocket that isn't coming — make the section you've got.
